title: Automate Created Subitem Dependency
excerpt: Hi, when I have an Automation that moves an Item to another Board, and then creates Subitems under that Items that was moved, I want the new Subitems Dependency automatically set as the dependent on the previous Subitem …

posts.author: IanR (Ian Rudman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, when I have an Automation that moves an Item to another Board, and then creates Subitems under that Items that was moved, I want the new Subitems Dependency automatically set as the dependent on the previous Subitem that was created. Is there any way to Automate to Set Dependency?

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is really complicated, especially with subitems. To be honest, I don’t fully understand the nuances of your issue, but you’ll have a hard time doing this with monday.com’s default automations.

One thing I can suggest is trying to use the duplicate group functionality. It can help when you’re trying to maintain dependencies even when duplicating. Perhaps you can use this as a kind of template.

In general, though, there are limited automations that work with subitems and even fewer that work with dependencies.

I’d consider the following:

  1. Do you definitely need to use subitems?
  2. Do you really need to move to a different board? (I find that monday.com users tend to want to use more boards when using fewer is usually superior)

The only other approach is to use a tool like make.com

posts.author: IanR (Ian Rudman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Francis, thank you for the reply. I will look at your considerations and attempt to put something together that will work with the available options, thank you!
